name: REST endpoint missing baseline security headers
description: Hits the target's root path with a plain GET, then asserts the response carries the minimum browser-protection headers. Missing headers indicate the server is shipping a default ASP.NET / Express / Spring response without security-headers middleware — exploitable via clickjacking (X-Frame-Options), MIME-sniffing (X-Content-Type-Options), and downgrade attacks (HSTS).

vulnerability.remediation:
Add a security-headers middleware before the routing pipeline:

ASP.NET Core — `app.UseSecurityHeaders()` (NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders) wired with Csp / Hsts / FrameOptions / ContentTypeOptions defaults.
Express.js — `app.use(helmet())`.
Spring Boot — `http.headers().contentSecurityPolicy("...").frameOptions().sameOrigin().contentTypeOptions().and().httpStrictTransportSecurity().includeSubDomains(true)`.

Validate via the same Bowire scan after the middleware lands — the finding should clear.
